description.content: Operations for listing, creating, and managing Power Platform environments. Environments are containers that store apps, flows, data, and other resources.

Retrieves a list of all Power Platform environments for the tenant. The response includes environment properties such as display name, location, SKU type, provisioning state, capacity, linked Dataverse metadata, and runtime endpoints. Requires tenant admin or Power Platform admin permissions.

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this request.
Comma-separated list of related properties to expand. Supported values include properties.capacity and properties.addons.

response.name: A list of environments for the tenant.

Operations for managing application packages within Power Platform environments, including listing available packages, installing applications, and checking installation status.

Retrieves the list of application packages available for installation in a specific Power Platform environment. Returns packages with their current installation status, version information, and metadata.

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the target environment.

Operations for monitoring and retrieving Power Automate flow run history within environments.

Retrieves flow runs by workflow ID for a specific Power Platform environment. Returns a list of flow run records including their status, start time, end time, and trigger information. The environment must have a Microsoft Dataverse database.

Operations for managing billing policies and licensing across the Power Platform tenant.
Retrieves the list of billing policies configured for the tenant. Billing policies define the association between Azure subscriptions and Power Platform environments for pay-as-you-go billing.

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this request.
The maximum number of billing policies to return.

response.name: A list of billing policies for the tenant.

Unified RESTful API for Microsoft Power Platform administrative capabilities including environment management, application package management, flow run monitoring, and licensing governance. Provides a single endpoint at api.powerplatform.com for programmatic access to Power Platform resources across tenants and environments.

Contact Support:
 Name: Microsoft Power Platform Support
